components.label: Browser/App Client
components.description: Chunked video upload; adaptive bitrate (ABR) video playback via CDN

components.label: API Gateway
components.description: Rate limiting, auth, upload session management, request routing

components.label: Load Balancer
components.description: Distributes upload and metadata API traffic across app server fleet

components.label: App Server
components.description: Handles upload initiation, metadata writes, search, recommendations API

components.label: Transcoding Queue (Kafka)
components.description: Receives raw upload events; dispatches parallel transcoding jobs per quality tier

components.label: Metadata DB (MySQL + Bigtable)
components.description: MySQL for video metadata and user data; Bigtable/Cassandra for watch history and activity

components.label: Cache (Redis + Memcached)
components.description: Video metadata cache, recommendation signals, view count aggregation buffer

components.label: Object Storage (GCS/S3)
components.description: Raw video uploads and all transcoded output variants (6+ quality tiers per video)

components.label: CDN
components.description: Global edge delivery for all video streams; supports HLS/DASH adaptive bitrate

components.label: Monitoring
components.description: Upload throughput, transcoding queue depth, CDN bandwidth, buffering ratio per CDN PoP

keyInsights: Chunked resumable uploads (GCS/S3 multipart) are mandatory — video files are too large for single-request uploads
Transcoding is a queue-worker fan-out: one raw video spawns 6+ parallel jobs (360p, 480p, 720p, 1080p, 1440p, 4K)
CDN delivers HLS/DASH manifests and video segments — the origin never streams directly to viewers
View count is a hot-write problem: use Redis counter + async flush to DB to avoid thundering-herd on viral videos

tradeoffs.pro: Scales elastically with upload volume; failure of one worker does not lose the job
tradeoffs.con: Transcoding delay before video is available (target: <5 min); queue depth spikes during upload bursts

tradeoffs.pro: Client switches quality tiers seamlessly based on bandwidth; reduces buffering
tradeoffs.con: Generates 6x storage per video; CDN must serve large numbers of small segment files

tradeoffs.pro: Origin servers serve zero video bytes in steady state; infinite scale at edge
tradeoffs.con: Cold cache on new/rare videos causes cache misses hitting origin; requires cache warming strategy

antiPatterns: Streaming video bytes from app servers — impossible to serve 10M concurrent streams from a fixed server fleet
Synchronous transcoding in the upload request cycle — 500 hrs/min would require infinite synchronous capacity
Storing view counts as single DB row updates — every view on a viral video causes DB lock contention
Single-region storage without CDN replication — global viewers experience unacceptable latency from origin
